data.title: Pfizer CEO criticizes RFK's vaccine views as anti-science, calls for US health leadership change. Bourla warns US falling behind China in health research.
data.description: Pfizer Inc.'s CEO, Albert Bourla, openly criticized Robert F. Kennedy for his anti-vaccine views, deeming them as "anti-science." Bourla emphasized the need for a change in leadership at the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services to make significant progress in immunization policies. During a Wall Street Journal event at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, Bourla stated that discussions with Kennedy always reached an impasse when vaccines were the topic, despite other healthcare exchanges being productive. He mentioned that talks on cancer research and pharmaceutical pricing were fruitful, contrasting starkly with the challenging nature of vaccine-related conversations. Bourla characterized Kennedy's perspective as inflexible and ideological, presenting it more as a belief system rather than a scientific discussion. The CEO's remarks were made during a Wednesday event and full coverage can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Shell is expanding in Nigeria with Bonga projects, potentially spending $20 billion, including $10 billion on Bonga South-West.
data.description: Shell PLC shares (NYSE: SHEL) saw an increase as NNPC Group CEO Bashir Ojulari mentioned the company's plan to invest up to $20 billion in Nigeria's oil and gas sector. Ojulari also discussed ongoing talks regarding the Bonga South-West project, which could involve about $10 billion in capital spending and substantial operating expenditure throughout its lifespan. Shell recently made a final investment decision of around $5 billion on the Bonga project.

data.title: Metropolitan Floors introduces RIFT, the latest Couture Collection, featuring white oak in straight lay, chevron, and parquet.
data.description: Kentwood Floors, based in Vancouver, British Columbia, has introduced a new product named RIFT as part of the Couture Collection. RIFT features rift-sawn white oak available in straight lay and chevron patterns, with parquet design set to be launched this spring.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures drop as Trump prepares Davos speech post Greenland sell-off; Netflix suffers plunge after earnings.
data.description: After the S&P 500 and Nasdaq dropped due to Trump's Greenland threats, he is set to speak at Davos. Netflix also suffered a decline in earnings. Dow Jones Futures are falling ahead of Trump's speech in Davos following the Greenland sell-off, with Netflix experiencing a significant plunge.

data.title: Supreme Court cautious on affecting Fed independence in current case; live updates available.
data.description: The independence of the Federal Reserve is being threatened in an ongoing case. The Supreme Court is currently showing caution in order to avoid any harm to the Federal Reserve's autonomy.

data.title: Opna Bio's OPN-2853 (Zavabresib) granted Orphan Drug Designation for myelofibrosis by FDA, a BET inhibitor.
data.description: In South San Francisco, Opna Bio, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company specializing in oncology therapeutics, has revealed that its Bet small molecule inhibitor, OPN-2853, has received Orphan Drug Designation from the FDA for the management of myelofibrosis. The generic name "zavabresib" has also been accepted internationally. This designation provides various benefits to assist in the development and commercialization of OPN-2853 for treating myelofibrosis, a condition affecting a specific number of people in the US.

data.title: NAR report: Dec pending home sales down 9.3%, 3.0% YOY.}/#{character limit reached}
data.description: In Washington, D.C., the National Association of REALTORS® reported a 9.3% drop in pending home sales in December compared to the previous month. Year over year, there was a 3.0% decrease in pending home sales. This data is crucial for agents, homebuyers, and sellers as it offers insights into the current state of the real estate market and the number of homes that are under contract.

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech plans rights offering; expects $12 million in proceeds if fully subscribed. Key dates and terms disclosed.
data.description: AIM ImmunoTech Inc., headquartered in Ocala, Florida, and listed on NYSE American as AIM, disclosed an update for its security holders about a proposed rights offering. The immuno-pharma company, dedicated to creating therapeutics for various cancers, immune disorders, and viral diseases, detailed the expected key dates and terms of the offering. If fully subscribed, the Rights Offering is anticipated to bring in gross proceeds of $12 million for AIM, after deducting expenses associated with the offering.

data.title: Lightning AI and Voltage Park merge to create AI cloud, catering to 400,000 developers, startups, offering GPU infrastructure.
data.description: Lightning AI, a leading cloud platform facilitating the development and execution of AI applications, has recently finalized a merger with Voltage Park, a substantial GPU infrastructure supplier. The amalgamated entity, now collectively identified as Lightning AI, integrates AI software with readily available GPU compute resources within a unified AI cloud dedicated to the training, deployment, and operation of AI models and applications. With a user base exceeding 400,000 comprising individual developers and startup organizations, the merger aims to enhance the accessibility and efficiency of AI technologies.

data.title: Inventor from Payson, Ariz. creates TEMPRA GLASS cover for broken vehicle windows, aiming to provide an improved solution.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 23, 2026, an inventor from Payson, Arizona shared his inspiration behind creating TEMPRA GLASS. He aimed to provide a better solution for temporarily covering damaged vehicle windows until repair or replacement is possible. With this innovative design, there is no longer a need for traditional makeshift coverings.

data.title: Real estate expert Joyce Nunes discusses coastal real estate in Leland, NC. Buyers must consider more than price and size.
data.description: In Leland, North Carolina on January 23, 2026, Joyce Nunes from Intracoastal Realty discusses the unique aspects of buying coastal real estate versus inland properties. Nunes highlights that buyers near the Intracoastal Waterway face considerations beyond just price and square footage. In an article in HelloNation, Nunes elaborates on the specific factors that differentiate purchasing property along the coast, emphasizing the need to navigate diverse elements when investing in coastal homes.

data.title: Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP if you suffered losses in CRWV securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025. Case filed against CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRWV) seeking recovery in securities fraud class action.
data.description: Did you experience investment setbacks in CRWV stocks from March 28 to December 15, 2025? A class action lawsuit has been initiated for investors who suffered losses in CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRWV) securities during this time frame. Affected purchasers or acquirers of CRWV stocks are invited to participate in seeking compensation for their investment losses.

data.title: Chéticamp long term care workers at Foyer Père Fiset vote 93% in favor of strike mandate for better wages and retention.
data.description: Employees at Foyer Père Fiset Long Term Care Home in Chéticamp, NS, and represented by CUPE 2031, have voted overwhelmingly in favor of a strike mandate at 93%. The primary demands include better wages, as their current rates are the lowest in Atlantic Canada, as well as improvements in recruitment and staff retention. CUPE 2031 President Trevor Poirier expressed the urgency for the government to engage in fair negotiations, emphasizing the need for Nova Scotians to be at the forefront rather than falling behind in terms of labor standards.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF flows are closely watched in digital asset markets, but price doesn't always move in sync. Flows reflect institutional behavior and are reported daily, influencing market demand. However, they don't dictate short-term price direction as institutions accumulate during consolidation. Retail traders must understand these dynamics to position themselves effectively. (Word count: 50)
data.description: Bitcoin spot exchange traded funds have been approved, unlocking institutional capital and reshaping the crypto market. ETF flows have become a crucial data point in digital asset markets, but they do not always align with price movements. Flows represent institutional behavior and show how institutions deploy capital, manage risk, and express conviction in Bitcoin. While ETF flows can impact the underlying market, they do not always dictate short-term price direction. Institutions often accumulate during consolidation periods and reduce exposure during strength, causing divergence between price and flow data. Sustained ETF inflows do not always lead to immediate price increases due to global trading, various factors can mute or delay the price impact of ETF inflows. Understanding this dynamic is crucial for traders looking to anticipate market moves rather than react to headlines.

data.title: Evolution Hospitality takes over management of Balfour Miami Beach, an 82-room boutique hotel in Miami's SoFi neighborhood.
data.description: Evolution Hospitality, a subsidiary of Aimbridge Hospitality known for creating unique lifestyle experiences and achieving top-notch performance, has taken over the management and operations of Balfour Miami Beach. This 82-room boutique hotel, situated in the upscale South of Fifth (“SoFi”) district in Miami Beach, Florida, is part of the Registry Collection Hotels by Wyndham Hotels & Resorts. The property was previously managed by FullG CRE Investments Inc.

data.title: Relay sees 461% growth, ranks #175 on Deloitte Fast 500, #920 on Inc. 5000, adds IPO-veteran leadership to expedite digitalizing physical economy.
data.description: Ranked at #175 on the Deloitte Technology Fast 500™ and #920 on the Inc. 5000, Relay, the Intelligent System of Action, has enhanced its leadership team with experienced IPO veterans. The company, based in Raleigh, N.C., is focused on accelerating the digital transformation of the physical economy through its innovative cloud-based platform.

data.title: IBM updates Qiskit to v2.3, including C API for Quantum-HPC integration, with faster optimization and fault-tolerant tools.
data.description: IBM has recently launched the Qiskit SDK version 2.3, which showcases custom C transpiler passes, accelerated circuit optimization, and tools for early fault-tolerant computing, aiming to aid quantum developers in their work. The latest update includes enhancements to streamline the development process, with a focus on faster processing. This release is significant as it brings improved capabilities to quantum computing, offering advanced tools to developers looking to leverage these technologies.
